title: cyperus ferax f. parvus
intro: Cyperus ferax f. parvus is a subspecies of the Cyperus ferax plant, commonly known as the "dwarf umbrella sedge" or "umbrella sedge," which is a type of aquatic or marshland plant belonging to the sedge family. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and compounds. The health benefits of Cyperus ferax f. parvus are said to include reducing inflammation and pain, as well as treating conditions such as eczema, wounds, and skin infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, which are applied directly to the affected area to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
